name: Maria Bonita Restaurant
tagline: 28-year Chinatown Mexican institution — BYOB, no AC, just fans and the Mexican flag on the walls. The wet chile verde burrito is legendary.
mustOrder: wet chile verde burrito — smothered in green sauce, the signature
ahi burrito — Hawaiian-Mexican fusion done right
parking: Street parking on Hotel St; paid lots on Maunakea St
talkStory: Owner Diego Giusti, originally from Mexico City, started slinging burritos from a lunch wagon in Waimanalo around 1998 and named the restaurant after his mother. The Chinatown storefront has ceiling fans instead of AC and a Mexican flag on the wall — Diego says it keeps things real. BYOB with no corkage is the move: regulars bring six-packs of Modelo. The wet chile verde burrito, smothered in tangy green sauce, has been the signature for over 25 years. Portions are enormous — one burrito genuinely feeds two.
